title: Planet Labs: The Satellite Stock That Keeps Shooting to the Moon
text:
Planet Labs has emerged as one of the most successful space technology companies, with stock returns exceeding 1,000% over the past year as the company demonstrates strong execution and growing demand for satellite intelligence. The company's fiscal 2026 results, released March 19, 2026, showcased record revenue, profitability achievement, and substantial backlog growth.

Record quarterly revenue of $86.8 million (41% YoY growth) and full-year revenue of $307.7 million (26% YoY growth) reflect strong market demand. The company's achievement of its first full year of non-GAAP profitability ($15.5 million adjusted EBITDA) represents a critical milestone for the space industry. Free cash flow of $52.9 million demonstrates the company's ability to fund growth initiatives internally.

The $900 million backlog, representing 79% year-over-year growth, provides substantial revenue visibility. With approximately 85% of revenue from annual or multi-year contracts and more than 90% from recurring subscriptions, the company has built a highly predictable revenue model. The Defense & Intelligence segment's 50%+ growth drives the backlog expansion, reflecting strong government demand.

Major contract wins with Sweden, Germany, and other nations demonstrate the company's appeal to international defense and security agencies. The NVIDIA partnership for in-orbit and ground-based AI processing enhances the value proposition of Planet Labs' services. Looking ahead, fiscal 2027 guidance of $415-440 million revenue suggests the company will continue to pull forward backlog while maintaining profitability. The combination of growth, profitability, and substantial backlog supports the market's enthusiasm for the company.
